content.full_text:
May 16, 1990

Cary Davidson
REED & DAVIDSON
888 West Sixth Street
12th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90017

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-89-576 90-356

Dear Mr. Davidson:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of a recent determination by the Commission concerning whether, under the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1, candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates. Specifically, you refer to our Davidson Advice Letter, No. I-89-576 (copy enclosed) in which we advised you that such contributions were not permitted under the Act.

At its meeting on March 13, 1990, the Commission reconsidered this advice and directed staff to advise that a candidate's contribution of campaign funds to a ballot measure committee by another candidate is permissible and does not violate Section 85304 of the Act. Enclosed for your reference is a letter to Thomas Hiltachk, dated March 16, 1990, which sets forth the Commission's position on this matter. To the extent that the advice given to you in advice letter No. I-89-576 is inconsistent with the March 16, 1990 letter to Mr. Hiltachk, the letter to Mr. Hiltachk now controls.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Should you have any questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Scott Hallabrin
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:SH:ld

Enclosures
